name: ckeyhasmap_view_unconstrained
reason: `CKeyHashMap::view` is an uninterpreted spec function on an external_body struct — the SMT solver has no axioms constraining it, so two distinct CKeyHashMaps could have identical or different views with no relation to their actual contents

name: clone_up_to_view_loses_marshalability
code:
proof fn phi_2_clone_up_to_view_loses_marshalability(x: CSingleMessage, y: CSingleMessage)
    requires
        x@ == y@,
        x is Message,
    ensures
        y is Message || y is Ack || y is InvalidMessage,
{
}
reason: `clone_up_to_view` external_body ensures only `c@ == self@` — but `CSingleMessage::Message{seqno: 0, ...}@` equals `CSingleMessage::Ack{ack_seqno: 0}@` is false only because view maps to different variants; however the clone could return a structurally different concrete variant (e.g. different Vec contents) that happens to have the same abstract view

name: seqno_nat_cast_loses_info
code:
proof fn phi_3_seqno_nat_cast_loses_info(s1: u64, s2: u64, dst: EndPoint, m: CMessage)
    requires
        s1 as nat == s2 as nat,
    ensures
        s1 == s2,
{
}
reason: The view function casts `seqno: u64` to `nat`, which is injective for u64 values — but if the spec were ever extended to allow larger sequence numbers, this identity would break; currently it's a tautology that hides the assumption that u64 is sufficient for sequence numbers

name: abstractify_cmessage_seq_invalid_preserved
code:
proof fn phi_4_abstractify_cmessage_seq_invalid_preserved(msgs: Seq<CSingleMessage>, i: int)
    requires
        0 <= i < msgs.len(),
        msgs[i] is InvalidMessage,
    ensures
        abstractify_cmessage_seq(msgs)[i] is InvalidMessage,
{
}
reason: `abstractify_cmessage_seq` faithfully maps InvalidMessage to abstract InvalidMessage — invalid/unparseable messages are carried through the abstraction layer rather than being filtered out, allowing protocol-level reasoning to accidentally include garbage messages in packet sequences

name: delegate_hashmap_view_not_injective
code:
proof fn phi_5_delegate_hashmap_view_not_injective(h1: CKeyHashMap, h2: CKeyHashMap, range: KeyRange<CKey>)
    requires
        h1@ == h2@,
    ensures
        (CMessage::Delegate{ range: range, h: h1 })@ == (CMessage::Delegate{ range: range, h: h2 })@,
{
}
reason: Two CKeyHashMaps with the same uninterpreted view produce the same abstract Delegate message — but since `CKeyHashMap::view` is uninterpreted, structurally different hashmaps could share a view, meaning the Delegate message abstraction conflates distinct concrete states that may behave differently during marshaling or delegation
